file_name: Cerus Corporation - FIRST AMEND TO SUPPLY AND MANUFACTURING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. Payment information, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice. Cerus shall have the right to accept or reject the Services, or any portion thereof, in writing within [*] days from Porex's completion of the Services. Such acceptance or rejection shall be consistent with the criteria set forth in the Statement of Work, if any.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. However, if Cerus is not satisfied with the performance of Porex, payments will be withheld until Cerus deems the service satisfactory. Payment information, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice. Cerus shall have the right to accept or reject the Services, or any portion thereof, in writing within [*] days from Porex's completion of the Services. Such acceptance or rejection shall be consistent with the criteria set forth in the Statement of Work, if any.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence 'However, if Cerus is not satisfied with the performance of Porex, payments will be withheld until Cerus deems the service satisfactory' creates a contradiction. The original clause states payment is due [*] days after invoice receipt, while the addition allows Cerus to withhold payment indefinitely based on subjective satisfaction. This introduces uncertainty in enforcement, since the original clause has a defined timeline while the added clause is vague and subjective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 1.5.3 shall be added to the Agreement as follows: 1.5.3 Porex will invoice Cerus monthly for Services performed by Porex during the prior month. Unless otherwise expressly provided in the applicable Statement of Work payment to Porex of undisputed fees shall be due [*] days following Cerus' receipt of the invoice submitted by Porex. However, if Cerus is not satisfied with the performance of Porex, payments will be withheld until Cerus deems the service satisfactory. Payment information, including Tax Identification Number will be included on each invoice. Cerus shall have the right to accept or reject the Services, or any portion thereof, in writing within [*] days from Porex's completion of the Services. Such acceptance or rejection shall be consistent with the criteria set forth in the Statement of Work, if any. If Cerus does not reject in writing within [*] days, the Services shall be considered accepted by Cerus. Within [*] days of any notice of rejection, Porex shall present a corrective plan of action to Cerus. Upon approval by Cerus of the corrective plan, Porex, at no additional expense to Cerus, shall then make the corrections and, where applicable, Porex shall resubmit the corrected Services to Cerus. For disputed invoices or the disputed portion of an invoice, Cerus shall use reasonable efforts to provide to Porex, in writing, within [*] days, a description of the disputed amounts. Cerus and Porex shall negotiate in a timely, good faith manner to resolve billing queries. Cerus will not have responsibility to pay for identified errors, incomplete or inaccurate items (collectively the "invoice errors") which result in increases to previously invoiced amounts if the aforementioned invoice errors are communicated to Cerus later than [*] days from the original invoice date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text states payment is due [*] days after invoice receipt. The added sentence, 'However, if Cerus is not satisfied with the performance of Porex, payments will be withheld until Cerus deems the service satisfactory', allows Cerus to withhold payment indefinitely based on subjective satisfaction. This contradicts the clearly defined payment timeline previously established and creates an inconsistency as to when payment is actually due, impacting the enforceability of the payment terms (Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction).

perturbation.original_text: In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Statement of Work, the Agreement shall control unless the Statement of Work expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Statement of Work and shall not be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Statement of Work, the Statement of Work shall control, irrespective of whether the Statement of Work expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Statement of Work and shall be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 5 completely reverses the order of precedence between the Agreement and a Statement of Work. Originally, the Agreement controlled unless explicitly stated otherwise in the Statement of Work. The modified text states that the Statement of Work always controls, effectively nullifying the original Agreement's terms whenever a conflict arises. This creates significant uncertainty and contradiction, especially concerning payment terms and obligations detailed elsewhere in the Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A new Section 3a shall be added to the Agreement as follows: "3a Non-Production Related Work. From time to time, Cerus may submit to Porex written work orders substantially in the form of Schedule 3 that specify development activities, engineering analysis, part, material and/or fixture design and development, or other services or activities that Cerus requests Porex to perform that are not activities or services that are necessary for the then-current manufacture and production of Components for Cerus under the Agreement (collectively, as defined in each work order, the "Services"). Each work order shall set forth the terms and conditions (including specifications (if applicable), delivery and performance schedules, and fees) pursuant to which Porex will perform the Services. Upon acceptance of a work order by Porex (in writing), such work order shall become a "Statement of Work." If Porex begins to perform services under a work order that has not been formally agreed in writing, then Porex shall bear the costs of any expenses incurred prior to formal written agreement of the Statement of Work. Cerus hereby consents to Porex's use of the Cerus Dedicated Space and Equipment, as specified in a Statement of Work, solely as necessary to perform the Services under that Statement of Work. Such consent shall automatically terminate upon the completion of the applicable Statement of Work and is limited by the terms of the Agreement. In the event of any conflict between the Agreement and a Statement of Work, the Statement of Work shall control, irrespective of whether the Statement of Work expressly refers to the Parties' intent to alter the terms of the Agreement with respect to that Statement of Work and shall be construed as an amendment to the terms of the Agreement."
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5 states that the Statement of Work shall control in the event of any conflict, irrespective of any explicit reference to altering the Agreement's terms. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which is the addition of new Section 3a, which also states, within itself, the same precedence rule. While the contradiction exists in the same piece of text, it is caused by the change in text overruling what the original text states. This creates a clear contradiction and ambiguity regarding the overarching agreement's terms and the precedence of Statement of Work, thus impacting contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: No other changes are made to the Agreement, including without limitation, the original Exhibit D, and the Agreement, the Plasma Disk pricing and related information as set forth in Exhibit D remain the same.
perturbation.changed_text: No other changes are made to the Agreement, except that the pricing for Plasma Disk set forth in Exhibit D is now subject to negotiation and may be adjusted based on market conditions, and the Plasma Disk pricing and related information as set forth in Exhibit D remain the same.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of 'except that the pricing for Plasma Disk set forth in Exhibit D is now subject to negotiation and may be adjusted based on market conditions' directly contradicts the second half of the sentence, which explicitly states 'the Plasma Disk pricing and related information as set forth in Exhibit D remain the same'. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding the stability and fixed nature of the Plasma Disk pricing, as outlined in Exhibit D.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No other changes are made to the Agreement, except that the pricing for Plasma Disk set forth in Exhibit D is now subject to negotiation and may be adjusted based on market conditions, and the Agreement, the Plasma Disk pricing and related information as set forth in Exhibit D remain the same. Except as amended herein, the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect. Capitalized terms not otherwise defined herein shall have the same meanings as set forth in the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text stated that the Plasma Disk pricing would remain the same as set forth in Exhibit D. The modified text then adds an exception allowing for negotiation and adjustment based on market conditions, while simultaneously stating that the pricing remains the same. The Contradicted text is the exact same as the Changed text. This directly violates the definition of Inconsistencies as it creates an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced, leading to disputes over contractual obligations.
